zh.lifeSummary: 當William de Radcliffe於1268年出生在英國藍卡爾時，他的父親Richard de Radcliffe23歲，母親Margaret le Boteler19歲。他和Margaret De Hindley至少有4個兒子和6個女兒。他去世於1347年在英國英格蘭藍卡爾博爾頓享年79歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当William de Radcliffe于1268年出生在英国藍卡爾时，他的父亲Richard de Radcliffe23岁，母亲Margaret le Boteler19岁。他和Margaret De Hindley至少有4个儿子和6个女儿。他去世于1347年在英国英格兰藍卡爾博爾頓享年79岁。

nameMeanings.name: Radcliffe
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Ratliff
meanings: English (Suffolk): habitational name from one or more of the places so named, such as Radcliffe (Lancashire), Radcliffe on Trent and Ratcliffe on Soar (both Nottinghamshire), Radclive (Buckinghamshire), Ratcliffe Culey and Ratcliffe on the Wreake (both Leicestershire), Ratclyffe in Clyst Hydon, Ratcliffes in Thorverton, and Ratcliffes in Broad Clyst (all Devon), Ratcliff in Stepney (Middlesex), and Rackley in Compton Bishop (Somerset). The placenames derive from Old English rēad ‘red’ + clif ‘cliff, bank, steep slope’ (see also Rutley ). A family of the name Radcliffe trace their descent from Sir Nicholas de Radclyffe. He is said to have been a knight who held the major of Radcliffe in Lancashire and served Roger de Poitou, Baron de Marsey in the 11th century.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of the various boys' names with the first syllable Ed-, especially Edward .

variants.name: Edward
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + weard ‘guard’. This has been one of the most successful of all Old English names, in frequent use from before the Conquest to the present day, and even being exported into other European languages. It was the name of three Anglo-Saxon kings and has been borne by eight kings of England since the Norman Conquest. It is also the name of the youngest son of Queen Elizabeth II . The most influential early bearer was King Edward the Confessor ( ?1002–66 ; ruled 1042–66 ). In a troubled period of English history, he contrived to rule fairly and (for a time at any rate) firmly. But in the latter part of his reign he paid more attention to his religion than to his kingdom. He died childless, and his death sparked off conflicting claims to his throne, which were resolved by the victory of William the Conqueror at the Battle of Hastings. His memory was honoured by Normans and English alike, for his fairness and his piety. Edward's mother was Norman; he had spent part of his youth in Normandy; and William claimed to have been nominated by Edward as his successor. Edward was canonized in the 12th century, and came to be venerated throughout Europe as a model of a Christian king.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When William de Radcliffe was born in 1268, in Lancashire, England, his father, Richard de Radcliffe, was 23 and his mother, Margaret le Boteler, was 19. He had at least 4 sons and 6 daughters with Margaret De Hindley. He died in 1347, in Bolton, Lancashire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 79.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque William de Radcliffe est né en 1268, à Lancashire, Angleterre, son père, Richard de Radcliffe, avait 23 ans et sa mère, Margaret le Boteler, avait 19 ans. Il a eu au moins 4 fils et 6 filles avec Margaret De Hindley. Il est décédé en 1347, à Bolton, Lancashire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 79 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als William de Radcliffe 1268 in Lancashire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Richard de Radcliffe 23 und seine Mutter Margaret le Boteler 19. Er hatte mit Margaret De Hindley mindestens 4 Söhne und 6 Töchter. Er starb 1347 im Alter von 79 Jahren in Bolton, Lancashire, England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Quando William de Radcliffe è nato il nel 1268 nel Lancashire, England, il suo padre, Richard de Radcliffe, aveva 23 anni e il suo madre, Margaret le Boteler, aveva 19 anni. Lui avuto almeno 4 figli e 6 figlie con Margaret De Hindley. Morì nel 1347, nel Bolton, Lancashire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 79 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: William de Radcliffe(이)가 1268년 영국 랭카실르에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Richard de Radcliffe(은)는 23세였고, 그의 어머니 Margaret le Boteler(은)는 19세였다. 그는 Margaret De Hindley(와)과 적어도 네 명의 아들과 여섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1347년 영국 잉글랜드 랭카실르 볼턴에서 79세의 나이로 사망했다.
